title: [firtool] No warning/error for ABI breaking module name
body:
```scala
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit Top:
  module ref_Top:
    input input: UInt<1>
    output x_probe: Probe<UInt<1>>

    define x_probe = probe(input)

  public module Top:
    input input: UInt<1>
    output inner_x_probe: Probe<UInt<1>>

    inst inner of ref_Top 
    connect inner.input, input
    define inner_x_probe = inner.x_probe
```
The example is very artificial but macro and module definitions are emitted in a same file. It's great that one of module or macro content was not erased but I think I can construct a mall-formed input that causes compilation error. I think we should use "-" as a delimiter "ref-Top.sv" (following layers) or emit proper errors.   

```
$ firtool top.fir -split-verilog -o design
$cat design/ref_Top.sv 
// Generated by CIRCT cd495f2a4
module ref_Top(
  input input_0
);

  wire input_probe = input_0;
endmodule

`define ref_Top_inner_x_probe inner.input_probe
```

Instance choice headers have a same problem.
code_blocks:
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit Top:
  module ref_Top:
    input input: UInt<1>
    output x_probe: Probe<UInt<1>>

    define x_probe = probe(input)

  public module Top:
    input input: UInt<1>
    output inner_x_probe: Probe<UInt<1>>

    inst inner of ref_Top 
    connect inner.input, input
    define inner_x_probe = inner.x_probe
$ firtool top.fir -split-verilog -o design
$cat design/ref_Top.sv 
// Generated by CIRCT cd495f2a4
module ref_Top(
  input input_0
);

  wire input_probe = input_0;
endmodule

`define ref_Top_inner_x_probe inner.input_probe
